translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The word of the LORD that came unto Hosea, the son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joash, king of Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The beginning of the word of the LORD by Hosea. And the LORD said to Hosea, Go, take unto thee a wife of whoredoms and children of whoredoms: for the land hath committed great whoredom, departing from the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So he went and took Gomer the daughter of Diblaim; which conceived, and bare him a son.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the LORD said unto him, Call his name Jezreel; for yet a little while, and I will avenge the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and will cause to cease the kingdom of the house of Israel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it shall come to pass at that day, that I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And she conceived again, and bare a daughter. And God said unto him, Call her name Loruhamah: for I will no more have mercy upon the house of Israel; but I will utterly take them away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah, and will save them by the LORD their God, and will not save them by bow, nor by sword, nor by battle, by horses, nor by horsemen.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Now when she had weaned Loruhamah, she conceived, and bare a son.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then said God, Call his name Loammi: for ye are not my people, and I will not be your God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured nor numbered; and it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people, there it shall be said unto them, Ye are the sons of the living God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then shall the children of Judah and the children of Israel be gathered together, and appoint themselves one head, and they shall come up out of the land: for great shall be the day of Jezreel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Say ye unto your brethren, Ammi; and to your sisters, Ruhamah.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Plead with your mother, plead: for she is not my wife, neither am I her husband: let her therefore put away her whoredoms out of her sight, and her adulteries from between her breasts;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Lest I strip her naked, and set her as in the day that she was born, and make her as a wilderness, and set her like a dry land, and slay her with thirst.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will not have mercy upon her children; for they be the children of whoredoms.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For their mother hath played the harlot: she that conceived them hath done shamefully: for she said, I will go after my lovers, that give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, mine oil and my drink.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore, behold, I will hedge up thy way with thorns, and make a wall, that she shall not find her paths.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And she shall follow after her lovers, but she shall not overtake them; and she shall seek them, but shall not find them: then shall she say, I will go and return to my first husband; for then was it better with me than now.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For she did not know that I gave her corn, and wine, and oil, and multiplied her silver and gold, which they prepared for Baal.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore will I return, and take away my corn in the time thereof, and my wine in the season thereof, and will recover my wool and my flax given to cover her nakedness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now will I discover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers, and none shall deliver her out of mine hand.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will also cause all her mirth to cease, her feast days, her new moons, and her sabbaths, and all her solemn feasts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will destroy her vines and her fig trees, whereof she hath said, These are my rewards that my lovers have given me: and I will make them a forest, and the beasts of the field shall eat them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim, wherein she burned incense to them, and she decked herself with her earrings and her jewels, and she went after her lovers, and forgat me, saith the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak comfortably unto her.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will give her her vineyards from thence, and the valley of Achor for a door of hope: and she shall sing there, as in the days of her youth, and as in the day when she came up out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it shall be at that day, saith the LORD, that thou shalt call me Ishi; and shalt call me no more Baali.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth, and they shall no more be remembered by their name.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And in that day will I make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven, and with the creeping things of the ground: and I will break the bow and the sword and the battle out of the earth, and will make them to lie down safely.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; yea, I will betroth thee unto me in righteousness, and in judgment, and in lovingkindness, and in mercies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will even betroth thee unto me in faithfulness: and thou shalt know the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And it shall come to pass in that day, I will hear, saith the LORD, I will hear the heavens, and they shall hear the earth;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the earth shall hear the corn, and the wine, and the oil; and they shall hear Jezreel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I will sow her unto me in the earth; and I will have mercy upon her that had not obtained mercy; and I will say to them which were not my people, Thou art my people; and they shall say, Thou art my God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then said the LORD unto me, Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress, according to the love of the LORD toward the children of Israel, who look to other gods, and love flagons of wine.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So I bought her to me for fifteen pieces of silver, and for an homer of barley, and an half homer of barley:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I said unto her, Thou shalt abide for me many days; thou shalt not play the harlot, and thou shalt not be for another man: so will I also be for thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For the children of Israel shall abide many days without a king, and without a prince, and without a sacrifice, and without an image, and without an ephod, and without teraphim:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Afterward shall the children of Israel return, and seek the LORD their God, and David their king; and shall fear the LORD and his goodness in the latter days.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear the word of the LORD, ye children of Israel: for the LORD hath a controversy with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no truth, nor mercy, nor knowledge of God in the land.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: By swearing, and lying, and killing, and stealing, and committing adultery, they break out, and blood toucheth blood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore shall the land mourn, and every one that dwelleth therein shall languish, with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven; yea, the fishes of the sea also shall be taken away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet let no man strive, nor reprove another: for thy people are as they that strive with the priest.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore shalt thou fall in the day, and the prophet also shall fall with thee in the night, and I will destroy thy mother.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge: because thou hast rejected knowledge, I will also reject thee, that thou shalt be no priest to me: seeing thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I will also forget thy children.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As they were increased, so they sinned against me: therefore will I change their glory into shame.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They eat up the sin of my people, and they set their heart on their iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And there shall be, like people, like priest: and I will punish them for their ways, and reward them their doings.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For they shall eat, and not have enough: they shall commit whoredom, and shall not increase: because they have left off to take heed to the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Whoredom and wine and new wine take away the heart.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My people ask counsel at their stocks, and their staff declareth unto them: for the spirit of whoredoms hath caused them to err, and they have gone a whoring from under their God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They sacrifice upon the tops of the mountains, and burn incense upon the hills, under oaks and poplars and elms, because the shadow thereof is good: therefore your daughters shall commit whoredom, and your spouses shall commit adultery.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will not punish your daughters when they commit whoredom, nor your spouses when they commit adultery: for themselves are separated with whores, and they sacrifice with harlots: therefore the people that doth not understand shall fall.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though thou, Israel, play the harlot, yet let not Judah offend; and come not ye unto Gilgal, neither go ye up to Bethaven, nor swear, The LORD liveth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For Israel slideth back as a backsliding heifer: now the LORD will feed them as a lamb in a large place.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim is joined to idols: let him alone.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Their drink is sour: they have committed whoredom continually: her rulers with shame do love, Give ye.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The wind hath bound her up in her wings, and they shall be ashamed because of their sacrifices.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Hear ye this, O priests; and hearken, ye house of Israel; and give ye ear, O house of the king; for judgment is toward you, because ye have been a snare on Mizpah, and a net spread upon Tabor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the revolters are profound to make slaughter, though I have been a rebuker of them all.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hid from me: for now, O Ephraim, thou committest whoredom, and Israel is defiled.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They will not frame their doings to turn unto their God: for the spirit of whoredoms is in the midst of them, and they have not known the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the pride of Israel doth testify to his face: therefore shall Israel and Ephraim fall in their iniquity; Judah also shall fall with them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall go with their flocks and with their herds to seek the LORD; but they shall not find him; he hath withdrawn himself from them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They have dealt treacherously against the LORD: for they have begotten strange children: now shall a month devour them with their portions.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Blow ye the cornet in Gibeah, and the trumpet in Ramah: cry aloud at Bethaven, after thee, O Benjamin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim shall be desolate in the day of rebuke: among the tribes of Israel have I made known that which shall surely be.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The princes of Judah were like them that remove the bound: therefore I will pour out my wrath upon them like water.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim is oppressed and broken in judgment, because he willingly walked after the commandment.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore will I be unto Ephraim as a moth, and to the house of Judah as rottenness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judah saw his wound, then went Ephraim to the Assyrian, and sent to king Jareb: yet could he not heal you, nor cure you of your wound.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I will be unto Ephraim as a lion, and as a young lion to the house of Judah: I, even I, will tear and go away; I will take away, and none shall rescue him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will go and return to my place, till they acknowledge their offence, and seek my face: in their affliction they will seek me early.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Come, and let us return unto the LORD: for he hath torn, and he will heal us; he hath smitten, and he will bind us up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: After two days will he revive us: in the third day he will raise us up, and we shall live in his sight.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Then shall we know, if we follow on to know the LORD: his going forth is prepared as the morning; and he shall come unto us as the rain, as the latter and former rain unto the earth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O Ephraim, what shall I do unto thee? O Judah, what shall I do unto thee? for your goodness is as a morning cloud, and as the early dew it goeth away.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore have I hewed them by the prophets; I have slain them by the words of my mouth: and thy judgments are as the light that goeth forth.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For I desired mercy, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: But they like men have transgressed the covenant: there have they dealt treacherously against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Gilead is a city of them that work iniquity, and is polluted with blood.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And as troops of robbers wait for a man, so the company of priests murder in the way by consent: for they commit lewdness.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have seen an horrible thing in the house of Israel: there is the whoredom of Ephraim, Israel is defiled.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Also, O Judah, he hath set an harvest for thee, when I returned the captivity of my people.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When I would have healed Israel, then the iniquity of Ephraim was discovered, and the wickedness of Samaria: for they commit falsehood; and the thief cometh in, and the troop of robbers spoileth without.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they consider not in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness: now their own doings have beset them about; they are before my face.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They make the king glad with their wickedness, and the princes with their lies.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are all adulterers, as an oven heated by the baker, who ceaseth from raising after he hath kneaded the dough, until it be leavened.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: In the day of our king the princes have made him sick with bottles of wine; he stretched out his hand with scorners.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For they have made ready their heart like an oven, whiles they lie in wait: their baker sleepeth all the night; in the morning it burneth as a flaming fire.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They are all hot as an oven, and have devoured their judges; all their kings are fallen: there is none among them that calleth unto me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim, he hath mixed himself among the people; Ephraim is a cake not turned.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Strangers have devoured his strength, and he knoweth it not: yea, gray hairs are here and there upon him, yet he knoweth not.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the pride of Israel testifieth to his face: and they do not return to the LORD their God, nor seek him for all this.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim also is like a silly dove without heart: they call to Egypt, they go to Assyria.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When they shall go, I will spread my net upon them; I will bring them down as the fowls of the heaven; I will chastise them, as their congregation hath heard.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Woe unto them! for they have fled from me: destruction unto them! because they have transgressed against me: though I have redeemed them, yet they have spoken lies against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And they have not cried unto me with their heart, when they howled upon their beds: they assemble themselves for corn and wine, and they rebel against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though I have bound and strengthened their arms, yet do they imagine mischief against me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They return, but not to the most High: they are like a deceitful bow: their princes shall fall by the sword for the rage of their tongue: this shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Set the trumpet to thy mouth. He shall come as an eagle against the house of the LORD, because they have transgressed my covenant, and trespassed against my law.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Israel shall cry unto me, My God, we know thee.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Israel hath cast off the thing that is good: the enemy shall pursue him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They have set up kings, but not by me: they have made princes, and I knew it not: of their silver and their gold have they made them idols, that they may be cut off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Thy calf, O Samaria, hath cast thee off; mine anger is kindled against them: how long will it be ere they attain to innocency?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For from Israel was it also: the workman made it; therefore it is not God: but the calf of Samaria shall be broken in pieces.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For they have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind: it hath no stalk: the bud shall yield no meal: if so be it yield, the strangers shall swallow it up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Israel is swallowed up: now shall they be among the Gentiles as a vessel wherein is no pleasure.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For they are gone up to Assyria, a wild ass alone by himself: Ephraim hath hired lovers.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, though they have hired among the nations, now will I gather them, and they shall sorrow a little for the burden of the king of princes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Because Ephraim hath made many altars to sin, altars shall be unto him to sin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have written to him the great things of my law, but they were counted as a strange thing.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They sacrifice flesh for the sacrifices of mine offerings, and eat it; but the LORD accepteth them not; now will he remember their iniquity, and visit their sins: they shall return to Egypt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For Israel hath forgotten his Maker, and buildeth temples; and Judah hath multiplied fenced cities: but I will send a fire upon his cities, and it shall devour the palaces thereof.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Rejoice not, O Israel, for joy, as other people: for thou hast gone a whoring from thy God, thou hast loved a reward upon every cornfloor.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The floor and the winepress shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail in her.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall not dwell in the LORD’s land; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and they shall eat unclean things in Assyria.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall not offer wine offerings to the LORD, neither shall they be pleasing unto him: their sacrifices shall be unto them as the bread of mourners; all that eat thereof shall be polluted: for their bread for their soul shall not come into the house of the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: What will ye do in the solemn day, and in the day of the feast of the LORD?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For, lo, they are gone because of destruction: Egypt shall gather them up, Memphis shall bury them: the pleasant places for their silver, nettles shall possess them: thorns shall be in their tabernacles.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The days of visitation are come, the days of recompence are come; Israel shall know it: the prophet is a fool, the spiritual man is mad, for the multitude of thine iniquity, and the great hatred.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The watchman of Ephraim was with my God: but the prophet is a snare of a fowler in all his ways, and hatred in the house of his God.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They have deeply corrupted themselves, as in the days of Gibeah: therefore he will remember their iniquity, he will visit their sins.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness; I saw your fathers as the firstripe in the fig tree at her first time: but they went to Baalpeor, and separated themselves unto that shame; and their abominations were according as they loved.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As for Ephraim, their glory shall fly away like a bird, from the birth, and from the womb, and from the conception.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though they bring up their children, yet will I bereave them, that there shall not be a man left: yea, woe also to them when I depart from them!

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim, as I saw Tyrus, is planted in a pleasant place: but Ephraim shall bring forth his children to the murderer.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Give them, O LORD: what wilt thou give? give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: All their wickedness is in Gilgal: for there I hated them: for the wickedness of their doings I will drive them out of mine house, I will love them no more: all their princes are revolters.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim is smitten, their root is dried up, they shall bear no fruit: yea, though they bring forth, yet will I slay even the beloved fruit of their womb.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: My God will cast them away, because they did not hearken unto him: and they shall be wanderers among the nations.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Israel is an empty vine, he bringeth forth fruit unto himself: according to the multitude of his fruit he hath increased the altars; according to the goodness of his land they have made goodly images.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Their heart is divided; now shall they be found faulty: he shall break down their altars, he shall spoil their images.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: For now they shall say, We have no king, because we feared not the LORD; what then should a king do to us?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They have spoken words, swearing falsely in making a covenant: thus judgment springeth up as hemlock in the furrows of the field.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The inhabitants of Samaria shall fear because of the calves of Bethaven: for the people thereof shall mourn over it, and the priests thereof that rejoiced on it, for the glory thereof, because it is departed from it.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It shall be also carried unto Assyria for a present to king Jareb: Ephraim shall receive shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As for Samaria, her king is cut off as the foam upon the water.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The high places also of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed: the thorn and the thistle shall come up on their altars; and they shall say to the mountains, Cover us; and to the hills, Fall on us.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O Israel, thou hast sinned from the days of Gibeah: there they stood: the battle in Gibeah against the children of iniquity did not overtake them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: It is in my desire that I should chastise them; and the people shall be gathered against them, when they shall bind themselves in their two furrows.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ephraim is as an heifer that is taught, and loveth to tread out the corn; but I passed over upon her fair neck: I will make Ephraim to ride; Judah shall plow, and Jacob shall break his clods.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap in mercy; break up your fallow ground: for it is time to seek the LORD, till he come and rain righteousness upon you.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ye have plowed wickedness, ye have reaped iniquity; ye have eaten the fruit of lies: because thou didst trust in thy way, in the multitude of thy mighty men.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people, and all thy fortresses shall be spoiled, as Shalman spoiled Betharbel in the day of battle: the mother was dashed in pieces upon her children.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: So shall Bethel do unto you because of your great wickedness: in a morning shall the king of Israel utterly be cut off.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: As they called them, so they went from them: they sacrificed unto Baalim, and burned incense to graven images.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I taught Ephraim also to go, taking them by their arms; but they knew not that I healed them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of love: and I was to them as they that take off the yoke on their jaws, and I laid meat unto them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He shall not return into the land of Egypt, but the Assyrian shall be his king, because they refused to return.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And the sword shall abide on his cities, and shall consume his branches, and devour them, because of their own counsels.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And my people are bent to backsliding from me: though they called them to the most High, none at all would exalt him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: How shall I give thee up, Ephraim? how shall I deliver thee, Israel? how shall I make thee as Admah? how shall I set thee as Zeboim? mine heart is turned within me, my repentings are kindled together.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will not execute the fierceness of mine anger, I will not return to destroy Ephraim: for I am God, and not man; the Holy One in the midst of thee: and I will not enter into the city.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall walk after the LORD: he shall roar like a lion: when he shall roar, then the children shall tremble from the west.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They shall tremble as a bird out of Egypt, and as a dove out of the land of Assyria: and I will place them in their houses, saith the LORD.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim compasseth me about with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit: but Judah yet ruleth with God, and is faithful with the saints.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim feedeth on wind, and followeth after the east wind: he daily increaseth lies and desolation; and they do make a covenant with the Assyrians, and oil is carried into Egypt.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The LORD hath also a controversy with Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways; according to his doings will he recompense him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He took his brother by the heel in the womb, and by his strength he had power with God:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yea, he had power over the angel, and prevailed: he wept, and made supplication unto him: he found him in Bethel, and there he spake with us;

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Even the LORD God of hosts; the LORD is his memorial.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: He is a merchant, the balances of deceit are in his hand: he loveth to oppress.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Ephraim said, Yet I am become rich, I have found me out substance: in all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that were sin.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And I that am the LORD thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I have also spoken by the prophets, and I have multiplied visions, and used similitudes, by the ministry of the prophets.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Is there iniquity in Gilead? surely they are vanity: they sacrifice bullocks in Gilgal; yea, their altars are as heaps in the furrows of the fields.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And Jacob fled into the country of Syria, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept sheep.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And by a prophet the LORD brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet was he preserved.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim provoked him to anger most bitterly: therefore shall he leave his blood upon him, and his reproach shall his LORD return unto him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: When Ephraim spake trembling, he exalted himself in Israel; but when he offended in Baal, he died.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: And now they sin more and more, and have made them molten images of their silver, and idols according to their own understanding, all of it the work of the craftsmen: they say of them, Let the men that sacrifice kiss the calves.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore they shall be as the morning cloud, and as the early dew that passeth away, as the chaff that is driven with the whirlwind out of the floor, and as the smoke out of the chimney.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Yet I am the LORD thy God from the land of Egypt, and thou shalt know no god but me: for there is no saviour beside me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I did know thee in the wilderness, in the land of great drought.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: According to their pasture, so were they filled; they were filled, and their heart was exalted; therefore have they forgotten me.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Therefore I will be unto them as a lion: as a leopard by the way will I observe them:

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will meet them as a bear that is bereaved of her whelps, and will rend the caul of their heart, and there will I devour them like a lion: the wild beast shall tear them.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O Israel, thou hast destroyed thyself; but in me is thine help.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will be thy king: where is any other that may save thee in all thy cities? and thy judges of whom thou saidst, Give me a king and princes?

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I gave thee a king in mine anger, and took him away in my wrath.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The iniquity of Ephraim is bound up; his sin is hid.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: The sorrows of a travailing woman shall come upon him: he is an unwise son; for he should not stay long in the place of the breaking forth of children.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will ransom them from the power of the grave; I will redeem them from death: O death, I will be thy plagues; O grave, I will be thy destruction: repentance shall be hid from mine eyes.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Though he be fruitful among his brethren, an east wind shall come, the wind of the LORD shall come up from the wilderness, and his spring shall become dry, and his fountain shall be dried up: he shall spoil the treasure of all pleasant vessels.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Samaria shall become desolate; for she hath rebelled against her God: they shall fall by the sword: their infants shall be dashed in pieces, and their women with child shall be ripped up.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: O Israel, return unto the LORD thy God; for thou hast fallen by thine iniquity.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Take with you words, and turn to the LORD: say unto him, Take away all iniquity, and receive us graciously: so will we render the calves of our lips.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Asshur shall not save us; we will not ride upon horses: neither will we say any more to the work of our hands, Ye are our gods: for in thee the fatherless findeth mercy.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely: for mine anger is turned away from him.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: I will be as the dew unto Israel: he shall grow as the lily, and cast forth his roots as Lebanon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: His branches shall spread, and his beauty shall be as the olive tree, and his smell as Lebanon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: They that dwell under his shadow shall return; they shall revive as the corn, and grow as the vine: the scent thereof shall be as the wine of Lebanon.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Ephraim shall say, What have I to do any more with idols? I have heard him, and observed him: I am like a green fir tree. From me is thy fruit found.

translations.name: King James Version
translations.text: Who is wise, and he shall understand these things? prudent, and he shall know them? for the ways of the LORD are right, and the just shall walk in them: but the transgressors shall fall therein.
